query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## US SPR Crude Oil — Current Level & Trajectory (June/July 2026)

**Current level:** The most recent EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1) shows **319.49 million barrels for the week ending July 3, 2026**. For June 2026 specifically, the SPR was declining every week — from ~340.3M (June 5) → ~325.7M (June 26, the lowest since 1983).

**Trajectory = sustained, accelerating RELEASE (not a fill):**
- **Driver:** An Iran war began end-February 2026. On March 11, the IEA agreed to a record 400M-barrel coordinated release; the US portion was **172 million barrels from the SPR**.
- **Draws began ~week of March 20, 2026.** DOE released 17.5M barrels total by late April (SPR at 397.9M), including a 7.1M single-week release (largest since Oct 2022).
- **Draws accelerated May–June:** 14 consecutive weekly draws through June 26, with several very large weeks (−8.9M, −9.05M, −5.5M, −6.17M).
- Net decline of ~78M barrels from ~397.9M (late April) to 319.5M (early July).

The SPR is now at its lowest level since 1983. Total US inventories (commercial + SPR) fell 111.4M barrels since the war began.

**One data caveat:** An automated extraction of the EIA weekly table suggested ~411–415M barrels for Dec 2025–Mar 2026 (pre-drawdown baseline). I flagged this magnitude as needing caution since it wasn't corroborated by a second source, though the qualitative pattern (flat before, sharp drawdown after late March) is confirmed across all sources.

sources.title: US Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve Stocks (Weekly)
sources.snippet: 319.49M bbl for Wk of Jul 03 2026, down from 325.66M last week and down from 402.76M one year ago.

sources.title: DOE has released 17.5 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve since March
sources.snippet: Between the week ending March 20 and the week ending April 24, DOE released a total of 17.5 million barrels; 7.1 million in week ending April 24. SPR stocks are currently 397.9 million barrels.

sources.title: Strategic Crude Oil Reserve Extends Drawdown to 14 Straight Weeks
sources.snippet: SPR recorded its 14th consecutive weekly draw during the week ended June 26; 5.5 MMbbl withdrawal reduced SPR inventories to 326 MMbbl, lowest since 1983.

sources.title: US emergency oil stockpile tumbles to lowest since the Reagan administration
sources.snippet: Officials released another 8.9 million barrels from the SPR last week, leaving the US emergency oil reserve with 340.3 million barrels of crude, taking out the prior low set in July 2023.

sources.title: US SPR falls to 325.7 million barrels, lowest since 1983
sources.snippet: SPR fell by 5.5 million barrels to 325.7 million barrels, the lowest level since May 1983, per DOE data.

sources.title: Oil stocks in US Strategic Petroleum Reserve fall to lowest since 1983
sources.snippet: Inventories fell by 9.05 million barrels, the third steepest draw on record. Part of a US agreement to release 172 million barrels.

sources.title: Strategic Petroleum Reserve (United States)
sources.snippet: As of June 26, 2026, the inventory was 325.7 million barrels. March 11, 2026: Trump authorized DOE to release 172 million barrels from the SPR, taking ~120 days.

sources.title: IEA Member countries to carry out largest ever oil stock release amid market disruptions from Middle East conflict
sources.snippet: 32 IEA member countries agreed to make 400 million barrels of oil from emergency reserves available to the market.

sources.title: Oil stocks in US SPR fall by 5.5 million to lowest level since 1983
sources.snippet: Since the war began at the end of February, overall US inventories including commercial and SPR stocks have fallen by 111.4 million barrels.

sources.title: Weekly U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in SPR (Thousand Barrels)
sources.snippet: EIA official weekly SPR crude oil stocks series (WCSSTUS1).
